<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fr:Ichikishimahime]][[ja:イチキシマヒメ]][[simple:Ichikishima-hime]][[zh:市寸島比賣命]]





{{Expand Japanese}}
{{Infobox deity
| type = Shinto
| father = [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]
| cult_centre = [[Munakata Taisha]]<br>[[Usa Jingū]]<br>{{ill|Matsunoo Taisha|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsunoo Taisha|qq=}}<br>{{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|en:draft|Yagi Shrine|fr|Yagi-jinja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|qq=}}
| siblings = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|en:draft|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タギツヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|en:draft|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}
|spouse={{ill|Amenohoakari|en|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|qq=}}|name=Ichikishimahime|other_names=Sayori-hime<ref name="web.archive.org-2016" /><ref name=". A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史" />|caption=Hetsu-gū (辺津宮) where she is worshipped in [[Munakata Taisha]]|image=File:Honden and haiden of Munakata Grand Shrine (Hetsu Shrine) 2.JPG|
| Hinduism_equivalent = [[Saraswati|Sarasvatī]]
| equivalent1_type = Buddhist
| equivalent1 = [[Benzaiten]]
| equivalent2_type = Japanese
| equivalent2 = {{ill|Seoritsu Hime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu Hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-hime|qq=}}
| gender = {{ill|female|qid=Q6581072|1=Female (gender)}}
| parents ={{ill|Susanoo|qid=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}; {{ill|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}| member_of = {{ill|Three Goddesses of Munakata|qid=Q10948069|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}
| equivalent5_type = Japanese | equivalent5 = {{ill|Benzaiten|qid=Q818468|1=Benzaiten}}; {{ill|Taionjin|qid=Q129820|1=Taionjin}}
}}

{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishimahime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishimahime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishimahime|qq=}} is a {{ill|Kunitsukami|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=}} she is one of the three daughters of [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] born by his challenge with [[Amaterasu]] who are worshipped at [[Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]].<ref name="Chamberlain">Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj020.htm Section XIII.—The August Oath.]</ref><ref name="Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner & Co.. [[Wikisource]]-1896">{{cite wikisource|author-first=William George|author-last=Aston|chapter=Book I|wslink=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697|plaintitle=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697|year=1896|publisher=Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner & Co.|wspage=35}}</ref><ref name=". A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史">{{Cite web|title=Ichikishimahime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/ichikishimahime/|access-date=2024-03-24|website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref><ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9397|access-date=2024-03-24|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref>

She was moved from [[Tashima Shrine (Saga)|Tashima Shrine]] to [[Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]], so that shrine is called Moto-Munakata.<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=wp4xhRXsARcC&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PA47|title=Fishing Villages in Tokugawa, Japan|last=Kalland|first=Arne|date=1995-01-01|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-1632-2|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=pcjZDwAAQBAJ&dq=Tashima+Shrine&pg=PT104|title=Sacred Heritage in Japan|last1=Rots|first1=Aike P.|last2=Teeuwen|first2=Mark|date=2020-04-02|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-000-04563-5|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=December 2023}}

She is also called {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishimahime|lt=Sayori-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishimahime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|en|6=en:draft|7=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishimahime|8=simple|9=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishimahime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishimahime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishimahime|qq=}},<ref name="web.archive.org-2016" /><ref name=". A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史" /> and is not to be confused with {{ill|Matsura Sayohime|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsura Sayohime|qq=}} who is said to be buried at [[Tashima Shrine (Saga)|Tashima Shrine]].<ref name="joya-1963a">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=daczAQAAIAAJ&q=%22Sayohime+shrine%22|title=Mock Jōya's Things Japanese|last=Jōya|first=Moku|date=1963|publisher=Tokyo News Service Press|page=222|authorlink=|qq=draft}}</ref><ref name="the-japan-office-editors-1915a">{{Cite magazine|date=1915|others=Based on material supplied by [[Shiga Shigetaka]]|title=September Sights: The Season of Festivals|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=h4o0AAAAIAAJ&q=+shrines+Tajima|magazine=Japan|publisher=The Japan Office|page=16|number=59}}</ref><ref name="murao-1968a">{{citation|last=Murao|first=Rikitarō|title=Tsukishi no no to Chikuhi no umi no kaiko: Nihon&kaigai shūkyō kōshō kenkyū|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=fOZCAQAAIAAJ&q=望夫石|journal=Wasada shōgaku|issue=205|page=103|year=1968|author-link=|script-title=ja:「筑紫の野」と「筑肥の海」の懐古―日本・海外宗教交渉略史研究―}}</ref>

She is worshipped at {{ill|Yagi Shrine|ja|夜疑神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|en|Draft:Yagi Shrine|fr|User:Immanuelle/Yagi-jinja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Shrine|qq=}} under the name {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishimahime|lt=Sayori-hime no Mikoto|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishimahime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|en|6=en:draft|7=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishimahime|8=simple|9=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishimahime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishimahime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishimahime|qq=}}.<ref name="web.archive.org-2016">{{Cite web|date=2016-08-18|title=��^�_�Ђ̌�Ր_|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160818202730/http://orange.zero.jp/hara.park/saijin.html|access-date=2024-03-23|website=web.archive.org}}</ref>

She was syncretized with many other deities in medieval times such as a younger sister of {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingu|1=Empress Jingū}}, or as [[Benzaiten]] or as a daughter of {{ill|Sagara (Hinduism)|lt=Sagara|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagara (Hinduism)|qq=}}.<ref name="國學院大學デジタルミュージアム" />

[[File:Okinoshima_Munakata_theology.svg|thumb|diagram explaining the structure of [[Munakata Taisha]] and its relationship to theology between the three sisters and [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] and {{ill|Watatsumi|en|Watatsumi|qid=Q1474187|lt=Watatsumi|1=Watatsumi}}]]

== Syncretism with Benzaiten ==
Due to her status as a water deity, {{ill|Benzaiten|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|qq=}} was also linked with ''{{ill|10=en|11=Nāgas|Nāga|lt=nāgas|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=en|qq=}}'', {{ill|10=en|11=Japanese_dragon|Dragon|lt=dragons|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=en|qq=}}, and {{ill|10=en|11=Snakes|Snake|lt=snakes|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=en|qq=}}.  Over time, Benzaiten became identified with the Japanese snake {{ill|10=en|11=Kami|kami|lt=''kami''|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=en|qq=}} {{ill|10=en|11=Ugajin|Ugajin|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=en|qq=}}. She also became identified with the ''kami'' {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishimahime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishimahime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishimahime|qq=}}.

Benzaiten was also adopted as a female ''{{ill|10=en|11=Kami|kami|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=en|qq=}}'' in {{ill|10=en|11=Shinto|Shinto|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=en|qq=}}, with the name {{nihongo|Ichikishimahime-no-mikoto|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}}.<ref name="bocking">{{cite book|title=A Popular Dictionary of Shinto - 'Benzaiten'|last=Bocking|first=Brian|publisher=Routledge|year=1997|isbn=978-0-7007-1051-5}}</ref> This kami is one of three ''{{ill|10=en|11=Kami|kami|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=en|qq=}}'' believed to be daughters of the sun goddess {{ill|10=en|11=Amaterasu|Amaterasu|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=en|qq=}}, the ancestress of the {{ill|10=en|11=Imperial_House_of_Japan|Imperial House of Japan|lt=imperial family|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=en|qq=}}.

She is also believed by {{ill|10=en|11=Tendai|Tendai|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=en|qq=}} Buddhists to be the essence of the ''kami'' {{ill|10=en|11=Ugajin|Ugajin|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=en|qq=}}, whose effigy she sometimes carries on her head together with a ''{{ill|10=en|11=Torii|torii|8=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=en|qq=}}'' (see photo below).<ref>{{EOS|Ugajin|200|Itō, Satoshi|August 15, 2011}}</ref> As a consequence, she is sometimes also known as {{nihongo|Uga|宇賀}} Benzaiten or Uga Benten.<ref name="Uga-Benzaiten">Ludvik, Catherine. “Uga-Benzaiten: The Goddess and the Snake.” Impressions, no. 33, 2012, pp. 94–109. JSTOR, JSTOR, www.jstor.org/stable/42597966.</ref>

[[File:Challenge_of_Amaterasu_and_Susanoo-eng.svg|thumb]]
{{Short description|Shinto goddess}}
{{Use dmy dates|date=September 2020}}

__FORCETOC__

== See Also ==

* {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|simple|User:Immanuelle/Munakata_clan|fr|Clan Munakata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|qq=}}

== Notes ==
{{notelist}}

== References ==
{{Reflist}}

== Bibliography ==
*Aoki, Michiko Y., tr. (1997). ''Records of Wind and Earth: A Translation of Fudoki, with Introduction and Commentaries''. Association for Asian Studies, Inc. {{ISBN|978-0924304323}}.
*Aston, William George, tr. (1896). ''Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697''. 2 vols. Kegan Paul. 1972 Tuttle reprint.
*Chamberlain, Basil H., tr. (1919). [http://www.sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/index.htm ''The Kojiki, Records of Ancient Matters'']. 1981 Tuttle reprint.
*{{cite journal |last1=Gadeleva |first1=Emilia |title=Susanoo: One of the Central Gods in Japanese Mythology |journal=Nichibunken Japan Review: Bulletin of the International Research Center for Japanese Studies |year=2000 |volume=12 |issue=12 |pages=168 |publisher=International Research Center for Japanese Studies|doi=10.15055/00000288|jstor=25791053 }}
*{{cite journal |last1=McMullin |first1=Neil |title=On Placating the Gods and Pacifying the Populace: The Case of the Gion "Goryō" Cult |journal=History of Religions |date=February 1988 |volume=27 |issue=3 |pages=270–293 |publisher=The University of Chicago Press|doi=10.1086/463123 |jstor=1062279 |s2cid=162357693 }}
*Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. {{ISBN|978-1400878000}}.

==External links==
{{Commonscat|Susano o no mikoto}}
*[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=143 Susanoo], Encyclopedia of Shinto
*[https://web.archive.org/web/20081205062552/http://www.uwec.edu/philrel/shimbutsudo/susano-o.html Susano-O no Mikoto], Kimberley Winkelmann, in the {{ill|Internet Archive|en|Internet Archive|qid=Q461|lt=Internet Archive|1=Internet Archive}} as of 5 December 2008
*[http://shinbutsu.jp/en/ Shaji Enza no Kai Organization]
*[https://www.susa-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Susa Shrine] (in Japanese)
*[http://www.yasaka-jinja.or.jp/ Official Website of Yasaka Shrine] (in Japanese)
*[http://www.kumanotaisha.or.jp/ Official Website of Kumano Taisha] (in Japanese)
*[http://musashiichinomiya-hikawa.or.jp/ Official Website of Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)] (in Japanese)
*[https://www.akasakahikawa.or.jp/ Official Website of Akasaka Hikawa Shrine] (in Japanese)
*[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=NVIBcAc4kxo Susanoo vs Yamata no Orochi] animated depiction

{{Munakata Faith}}

{{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}}
<!--removing this template because now we do not need it. All everybodywiki edit histories have been imported-->
}}

}

{{translated page|ja|イチキシマヒメ|version=102838069|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{citation needed|{Ichikishimahime

{{draft categories|

 

}}
{{everybodywiki link|Ichikishimahime|Ichikishimahime}}

[[simple:User:Ichikishima-hime]]

[[fr:Ichikishimahime]]
[[ja:イチキシマヒメ]]

[[zh:市寸島比賣命]]
[[fr:User:Ichikishima-hime]]

<!--all this actually mandates is a completion tier 7 ranking. We are not using the promising draft template here. If I get anywhere near implementing it, I will do so based on the actual completion tiers of this wiki rather than whether I added them when they were on simple english wikipedia or wherever--><!--城谷 黙 (1884-1963) --><!--11–20--><!--早稲田商学/早稻田商學--><!--93–--><!--村尾力太郎--><!--Ichikishimahime--><!--Ichikishimahime--><!--Ichikishimahime--><!--Ichikishimahime-->

<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis-->
{{Commons category|Ichikishima-hime}}
<!--zhwiki derived wikidata interwikis-->
{{Commons category|Ichikishima-hime}}
{{wikidata link|Q11059454}}
[[Category:Goddesses]]
[[Category:Hachiman faith]]
[[Category:Japanese deities]]
[[Category:Kunitsukami]]
[[Category:Sea and river deities]]
[[Category:War deities]]
[[Category:Water deities]]
[[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
